definition: A taxonomy classifying vol transitions into three categories: (a) surprise, (b) telegraphed-wrong-footed (direction known but positioning incorrect), and (c) telegraphed-correctly-positioned. Superior to binary surprise/telegraphed framework. The December 2018 analogue illustrates category (b). Current environment classified as most resembling category (c).
evidence: Identified as 'clearly stronger' than binary framework in pair_0 debate
December 2018 analogue captures real distinction of telegraphed direction with wrong-footed positioning
Classification of current environment as category (c) is important correction to rates vol underpricing thesis
open_questions: Can the taxonomy be used for prediction with only an 8-episode sample?
Is ex-post classification reliable enough for forward-looking assessment?
How do commodity-triggered transitions (inherently surprise) interact with this framework?
